findings.evidence: grep shows the lemma at line 54 with no @[simp] annotation; fiberHomeomorphMonoidHom_apply (line 42, @[simp]) rewrites MonoidHom→fiberHomeomorph (wrong direction) so cannot close fiberHomeomorph 1 b.
findings.issue: fiberHomeomorph_one is a normal-form lemma (fiberHomeomorph 1 b = 1) but lacks @[simp], and no other @[simp] lemma rewrites fiberHomeomorph 1 b.

findings.evidence: grep across the whole repo: both lemmas appear only at their definition sites in DeckFiberAction.lean and in the diff; no consumer references them.
findings.fix: Remove both lemmas. Users can write (mem_stabilizer_fiber_iff …).mp / .mpr.
findings.issue: coe_eq_of_mem_stabilizer_fiber (line 125) and mem_stabilizer_fiber_of_coe_eq (line 130) are exactly .mp and .mpr of mem_stabilizer_fiber_iff (line 119); neither is used elsewhere and both bloat the API surface with no independent content.

summary: Missing @[simp] on fiberHomeomorph_one (a normal-form lemma with no other simp pathway), and two redundant lemmas (coe_eq_of_mem_stabilizer_fiber, mem_stabilizer_fiber_of_coe_eq) that are just .mp/.mpr of the existing iff lemma and should be removed to keep the surface minimal.
